title: Alaska side quest: Glacier Bay
subtitle: A beautiful gloomy day cruising through glaciers

20250430_01.alt: A gloved hand holds up a map of Glacier Bay National Park, with the park in the background (water and mountains). It's a gloomy day.

20250430_02.alt: Landscape photo of snow capped mountains and some low clouds in the foreground

20250430_03.alt: Snow capped mountains, with the railings of a cruise ship on the left foreground

20250430_04.alt: A shot of a cruise ship deck looking straight down into the water, which is dappled with ice chunks

20250430_05.alt: Portrait-oriented photo of snowy mountains and a cruise ship cutting through the water

20250430_06.alt: Craggy mountain with snow, and ice chunks in the water in the foreground. Feels dark, cloudy, and moody

20250430_07.alt: Me, wearing an orange beanie and warm sweats looking out at the mountains and water behind the safety of a railing

20250430_08.alt: Me, wearing an orange beanie and turquoise eyeglasses flashing a peace sign and a slight smile at the camera, with snowy mountains and water in the background

20250430_09.alt: Rocky looking mountains with some snow, obscured partially by fog in the foreground

20250430_10.alt: Perspective lines on the edge of a cruise ship draw the eye toward an observation tower, with huge mountains

20250430_11.alt: A big chunk of ice floating in the blue-green water which has ripple lines

20250430_12.alt: Portrait-oriented photo of mountains of rock with some snow on them, reflected in the glassy water below, which has ice chunks stippled throughout

20250430_13.alt: Johns Hopkins Glacier from a distance, with many low ominous clouds above
20250430_13.caption: Johns Hopkins Glacier is over one mile wide, which is hard to appreciate from a photo

20250430_14.alt: Mountain spotted with snow and reflected in the water below

20250430_15.alt: Me, smiling and taking a full body selfie in the reflection of the cruise cabin window. I'm ecstatic at the beautiful mountain and water behind me.

20250430_17.alt: A snapshot of video from the live feed camera mounted on top of the cruise ship - the bow of the ship is visible, with about 50 passengers crowded on the deck taking photos of the mountains in the distance, which are gargantuan compared to the humans
20250430_17.caption: Cabins had live feed video from a camera mounted on top of the ship - see how small the humans are compared to the mountains

20250430_16.alt: Yet another moody shot of the mountains and water with clouds (did I take too many of these photos??)

20250430_20.alt: Johns Hopkins Glacier, from a distance. The face of the glacier is about one mile wide, a scale that's hard to fully appreciate from the photo

20250430_21.alt: Blue-green water with many ice chunks

20250430_22.alt: Closer photo of a very craggy rock

20250430_23.alt: Johns Hopkins Glacier sitting between two mountains

20250430_24.alt: Part of a glacier, with thousands of years of rock and silt streaking through the pure blue glacial ice

20250430_25.alt: View of one of the inlets to Glacier Bay from a bit of a distance

20250430_26.alt: Selfie of me doing a peace sign with the waters of Glacier Bay in the distance

blurb: A highlight of the Alaska cruise was the day the ship went through Glacier Bay. We were not allowed off the ship (nowhere to dock and honestly humans would ruin the park), but instead the national park rangers boarded the ship and were available for questions, which was neat.
